body:
Hey Ashley,

Just a reminder to get the weekly Bell Modern update out by end of day Friday. I know it's been a hectic week, but Jenna and the consignors like having it before the weekend, especially with the Modernism Week tour bookings ramping up.

Please don't skip the consignment summary this time — last week's was left blank and Megan had questions about whether the Kagan sofa actually sold.

Let me know if you want me to look it over before it goes out.

Karen
snippet:
Hey Ashley,

Just a reminder to get the weekly Bell Modern update out by end of ...
subject: Weekly Bell Modern Update Reminder

body:
Hi Karen,

Thanks for the nudge. I've got most of it drafted already — just need to fill in the 1stDibs numbers from Thursday and write up the consignment summary. It'll be out well before Friday.

On the summary, I'll flag that we're still waiting on Dave's sign-off on the two Danish teak chairs in the restoration backlog. They've been with him since Tuesday and it's holding up two listings. Want me to note that separately, or is the update enough?

Ashley
snippet:
Hi Karen,

Thanks for the nudge. I've got most of it drafted already — just need...
subject: Re: Weekly Bell Modern Update Reminder

body:
Hi Ashley, are you free for lunch this week? Thursday or Friday would both work on my end. I keep thinking about The Copper Table — the room is quiet enough to actually hear each other, and it's been too long since we caught up properly.

Let me know what your schedule looks like and I'll work around it.

Karen
snippet: Hi Ashley, are you free for lunch this week? Thursday or Friday would both work...
subject: Lunch this week?

body:
Thursday works. Noon at The Copper Table. I may run a few minutes late off the trail but I'll be there by 12:05.

Ash
snippet: Thursday works. Noon at The Copper Table. I may run a few minutes late off the trail but I'll
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
Let's say 12:30 to give you a buffer after the canyon — no rush on my end. The Copper Table is perfect; their back room is usually calm at lunch. If they're full I'm happy to walk over to Mariposa Bistro instead.

It really has been too long. Looking forward to it. See you Thursday.

Karen
snippet: Let's say 12:30 to give you a buffer after the canyon — no rush on my end. The C...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
Hi Ashley, I've finished the first pass on the provenance review for the Nakashima coffee table and I'd love to walk you through it before you list it. There are three threads I want to flag — the original bill of sale looks solid, the gallery label is consistent with the period, but there's a gap in the ownership chain in the late '70s I want to talk through.

Would you have 30–45 minutes this week or early next for a walkthrough? I can send over my provenance file ahead of time or just talk it through live. Let me know what works best.
snippet: Hi Ashley, I've finished the first pass on the provenance review for the Nakashi...
subject: Provenance Review – Nakashima Coffee Table

body:
Hi Karen, this is exactly why I wanted you to look at it before it went up — that '70s gap is the kind of thing 1stDibs buyers ask about. A walkthrough would be ideal so I can ask questions in real time.

I'm free Thursday afternoon after 2pm or anytime Friday morning. Does either of those work for you? Looking forward to seeing what you've put together.
snippet: Hi Karen, this is exactly why I wanted you to look at it before it went up — tha...
subject: Re: Provenance Review – Nakashima Coffee Table

body:
Thursday at 2:30pm works perfectly for me. I'll send a calendar invite shortly.

I'm also going to send over my provenance file ahead of time so you have the documentation in front of you — the bill of sale, the gallery label photos, and the auction record I dug up. That way we can use the call to talk through the '70s gap rather than setup. Let me know if you'd rather I hold off and just walk through everything live instead.
snippet: Thursday at 2:30pm works perfectly for me. I'll send a calendar invite shortly....
subject: Re: Provenance Review – Nakashima Coffee Table

body:
Thursday at 2:30 is confirmed on my end — thank you for sending the invite so quickly.

Please do send the provenance file ahead of time, that would be really helpful. I like being able to sit with the documentation a bit before we talk through it. See you Thursday!

Ashley
snippet: Thursday at 2:30 is confirmed on my end — thank you for sending the invite so qu...
subject: Re: Provenance Review – Nakashima Coffee Table

body:
Hey Ash! I've been looking at dates for Joshua Tree and I think the weekend of July 18th works best for me. I checked and there are still campsites available at Jumbo Rocks if we book soon.

I was thinking we drive out Friday evening after work, spend two full days hiking, and head back Sunday night? Let me know if that timeline works for you. Also wondering if we should just take my car since it has the roof rack for the gear.

Karen
snippet: Hey Ash! I've been looking at dates for Joshua Tree and I think the weekend of J...
subject: Trip logistics - Joshua Tree next month

body:
July 18th works. I can leave by 4 Friday. Your car makes sense with the rack — I'll cover gas, and yes, split Jumbo Rocks 50/50 ($25/night).

Pack our own most nights, one dinner in town. Send me the village spot.

Ash
snippet: July 18th works. I can leave by 4 Friday. Your car makes sense with the rack — I'll cover
subject: Re: Trip logistics - Joshua Tree next month

body:
Splitting the campsite sounds fair! And yes, let's do one dinner out — that place you found looks amazing, I actually saw it on Instagram last week.

I'll book the campsite today before it fills up. Can you handle the dinner reservation? I'm thinking Saturday night after we do the Skull Rock loop.

Also do you still have the portable stove from your camping bin? I couldn't find mine last time I looked.

Karen
snippet: Splitting the campsite sounds fair! And yes, let's do one dinner out — that plac...
subject: Re: Trip logistics - Joshua Tree next month

body:
Saturday 7pm reservation is set. I've got the stove, cooler, and chairs; you grab firewood off the 62. I'll send a packing list this week so we don't double up.

Ash
snippet: Saturday 7pm reservation is set. I've got the stove, cooler, and chairs; you grab firewood
subject: Re: Trip logistics - Joshua Tree next month

body:
Firewood is on my list, no problem! And yes please send the packing list — last time we ended up with four headlamps and zero sunscreen between us.

I'll pick you up at 4:15 Friday the 18th. Pack light if you can, I want to leave room in the back for the cooler and the big duffels without it being a game of Tetris the whole drive.

Can't wait. Only three and a half more weeks!

Karen
snippet: Firewood is on my list, no problem! And yes please send the packing list — last ...
subject: Re: Trip logistics - Joshua Tree next month

body:
Hi Ashley,

The storage unit renewal notice came in the mail today — our lease on the overflow unit is up next month and they've bumped the rate again. Before you just auto-renew, I think it's worth a look. We've crammed a lot more inventory in there since we first signed on and I'm not sure the current unit is even the right size anymore.

Could we sit down for 20 minutes this week or early next and go over it? Might be cheaper to size up than to keep paying overage.

Karen
snippet:
Hi Ashley,

The storage unit renewal notice came in the mail today — our lease ...
subject: Storage Unit Renewal – Overflow Inventory

body:
Hi Karen,

Good catch — I'd been ignoring that envelope. You're right, we're paying overage every month and it's getting silly. Sizing up to the next unit might actually come out cheaper, and it'd make pulling pieces for restoration a lot less painful.

Let's go over it. I'm free Thursday afternoon or anytime Friday morning — tell me what works and I'll block the time. I'll dig out last year's invoice so we have the numbers in front of us.

Ashley
snippet:
Hi Karen,

Good catch — I'd been ignoring that envelope. You're right, we're pay...
subject: Re: Storage Unit Renewal – Overflow Inventory

body:
Hey Ashley, I wanted to talk through the Shopify launch plan before you commit to a date. Jenna's proposing we flip the shop live the first week of September and migrate the active 1stDibs listings over the weekend before. That's a tight window, and I want to make sure we have a way to back out if the product photos or the provenance descriptions aren't ready.

My main worry is timing — that's right when Modernism Week tour bookings start coming in. Can you check whether any consignment or 1stDibs commitments would make that weekend a bad time to be heads-down on the shop?

Karen
snippet: Hey Ashley, I wanted to talk through the Shopify launch plan before you commit t...
subject: Shopify launch plan - thoughts?

body:
Hi Karen, good call flagging this early. I checked the calendar and we have two consignors — Megan's pieces and one of the local designers — with listings going up that same week, plus the 1stDibs end-of-month settlement. We'd want to give the consignors a heads-up at least 72 hours ahead so nothing gets pulled mid-migration.

I'd suggest we target Sunday for the actual switchover instead of mid-week, which is cleaner for everyone. I can draft the launch checklist to circulate tomorrow if you give the green light. Worth confirming with Jenna whether the product-photo shoot is finished or if that's still a separate step.

Ashley
snippet: Hi Karen, good call flagging this early. I checked the calendar and we have two ...
subject: Re: Shopify launch plan - thoughts?

body:
Sunday works much better — let's go with that. I just heard back from Jenna and yes, the product-photo shoot wraps Friday, so the images will be ready in time.

Please go ahead and draft the launch checklist. Make sure it covers the listing migration and a note to the consignors that their pieces may be briefly unlisted during the switchover. I'll review it before it goes out.

Karen
snippet: Sunday works much better — let's go with that. I just heard back from Jenna and ...
subject: Re: Shopify launch plan - thoughts?

body:
Hi Ashley,

Update on the Kagan credenza. The veneer on the top is worse than I thought once I got it stripped down - two spots I didn't see under the old finish that need patch work, and the grain match is going to take a couple extra sessions to get right. I'm now looking at Mar 4 for return, not the Feb 18 we had on the schedule.

Nothing structural, just the finish work needing more time. Let me know if that's a problem given the buyer situation and I'll see what I can do to compress.

Dave
snippet: Update on the Kagan credenza. The veneer on the top is worse than I thought once I got it stripped down...
subject: Kagan credenza - return date update

body:
Ashley,

Provenance work on the Paul Evans Cityscape cabinet came back stronger than I expected. Two of the recent auction hammers I pulled - Wright Chicago in November and Rago in December - both cleared north of $30,000 for comparable Cityscape pieces in the same welded-steel-and-slate program, with more surface wear than ours has. Given the condition and the paper trail on our cabinet, I'm moving the current market comp from the $22,500 we've been carrying to $32,000. I'd like that reflected in the top-of-schedule summary before the underwriter packet goes over.

I can send the auction result PDFs if you want them in the file.

Karen
snippet: Provenance work on the Paul Evans Cityscape cabinet came back stronger than I expected...
subject: Paul Evans Cityscape cabinet - revised comp

body:
Ashley,

Ahead of our renewal call next month, a quick note on where we are on the schedule.

Your current policy carries a scheduled personal property ceiling of $400,000 across the inventory line. That figure has been on the books since the last renewal and it is what underwriting will price the premium against unless we bring them a revised schedule that sits meaningfully above or below.

For the renewal packet we need:
  - The current reconciled schedule as a single running total.
  - The top of schedule pieces with the provenance summary attached to each.
  - A valuation line the underwriter can defend against comparable market activity.

If your reconciled schedule has drifted below the $400,000 ceiling by any material margin, we will want to walk that gap into the packet as coverage headroom rather than have the underwriter surface it on the call. The reverse is also true - if the reconciled figure has climbed above $400,000 the delta shows as coverage shortfall and we will need to schedule the additional value in.

Send the packet across when it is stranger-readable and I will pass it up the chain.

Best,
Laura Hayes
Senior Underwriter, Ardent Insurance
snippet: Ahead of our renewal call next month, a quick note on where we are on the schedule. Your current policy carries a scheduled personal property ceiling of $400,000 across the inventory line...
subject: Ardent renewal - scheduled personal property ceiling at $400,000

body:
Ashley,

Working through the reconciled schedule this afternoon against the pieces that have actually cleared the floor and the pieces still sitting in inventory. Where I have landed as of tonight:

  - 68 pieces on the reconciled schedule (down from 74 last quarter after the four Q4 sales     and the two struck-through Danish teak lots).
  - Running total on the reconciled schedule: ~$340,000 at current market comps.
  - Top of schedule (five pieces above $18k each) accounts for a little over a third of the     total: Vladimir Kagan Serpentine sofa, Paul Evans Cityscape cabinet at the revised     comp we discussed, Nakashima-style walnut credenza, George Nakashima Conoid bench,     Charlotte Perriand pine cabinet.

The Cityscape cabinet swap I flagged earlier moves us $9,500 up from the previous running total. The Kagan credenza is not on the schedule until Dave releases it back to the floor.

If this is the figure you want to walk into the Ardent packet let me know and I will lock the provenance summaries against these 68 rows so the packet reads cleanly.

Karen
snippet: Working through the reconciled schedule this afternoon against the pieces that have actually cleared the floor and the pieces still sitting in inventory. Where I have landed as of tonight: 68 pieces on the reconciled schedule...
subject: Reconciled schedule - 68 pieces at ~$340,000
